properties.name: All board content must come from deterministic renderers
properties.description: Graph-derived board content must be produced exclusively by TypeScript renderer functions; hand-written Excalidraw JSON in production paths is forbidden to guarantee idempotent round-trips between the graph and canvas.

properties.name: Tool must not be containerised
properties.description: The analyst tool shells out to the local claude CLI via itsalt-pinch; containerising it would sever that subprocess path, so the tool must always run directly on the analyst machine.

properties.name: Project registry is the single source of truth for active project
properties.description: ~/.nacl/projects.json is the sole authority for active project root and ID; the config manager reads it as a fallback after env overrides, ensuring no project identity is derived from ad-hoc sources.

properties.name: All claude -p subprocess calls must go through itsalt-pinch Pacer
properties.description: Direct child_process.spawn of claude is forbidden; all headless claude -p invocations must use the singleton Pacer so that global/per-project concurrency caps, working-window scheduling, and event hooks are consistently applied.

properties.name: Board name must match /^[A-Za-z0-9][A-Za-z0-9_-]*$/
properties.description: User-supplied board names are validated against a strict alphanumeric-plus-dash-underscore pattern to prevent path traversal and shell injection in all routes that accept a board name.

properties.name: Project ID must match /^[a-z0-9_-]{1,64}$/
properties.description: Project IDs are sanitised to Pinch invariant (lowercase, 1-64 chars, only alphanumeric/dash/underscore) when read from any source, ensuring claude -p projectId parameters are always safe.

properties.name: Loaded scene must have type === "excalidraw"
properties.description: When a board file is read via GET /api/v1/boards/:name, the parsed JSON must carry type=excalidraw; any other value triggers a 422 to prevent the editor from silently loading corrupt or misidentified files.

properties.name: Skill kind must be one of regenerate / sync / analyze / render-md
properties.description: Only the four whitelisted skill kinds are accepted; the TypeScript union type and runtime switch prevent raw user input from ever reaching a claude -p prompt, eliminating prompt injection via the skill API.

properties.name: render-md subtype must be one of six whitelisted values
properties.description: The markdown renderer dispatcher accepts only entity / uc / form / domain-model / uc-index / traceability; any other subtype throws an error, ensuring only known renderers execute.

properties.name: Boards of kind import or other cannot be regenerated
properties.description: The board classifier guards the regenerate path: import boards use sync instead and other-kind boards have no renderer, so regenerating either would silently produce nothing or overwrite hand-authored content.
